raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I booked a tour using the XXXX website for a ticket, guide, and travel to XXXX XXXX. I selected XX/XX/year> as the tour date, and the system let me book. On receiving the confirmation email, I noticed that it had confirmed a tour on XX/XX/XXXX when I would not be in the country. 

I quickly rang XXXX to report the issue and ask for a refund. They were particularly unhelpful. They recommended that I call the tour operator in XXXX ( an expensive long-distance call ) to try to cancel. 

When speaking to the tour operator, they confirmed they had no tickets available that day and could only provide the requested service on XX/XX/XXXX. I made sure the tour operator canceled this reservation, and they incurred no charges. 

After rounds of unsuccessful communication with Citibank on this, I reached out to the provider to confirm in writing that this charge was not valid. 

The attached conversation is with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) who is the tour operator that was providing the tour. XXXX is a third party operator who schedules tours with local operators. 

The tour operator has confirmed that XXXX 's system had incorrectly booked the tour and that I should be refunded. This was also relayed to the XXXX team, who has been unresponsive since this was once again raised to them on XX/XX/year>.
